entries.objective: DEEP DIVE: mathcrew Frontend/Ontology/Backend full audit + revolutionary improvement research. Focus areas: (1) 3D rendering quality breakthrough, (2) education-specialized tech stack, (3) content delivery paradigm shift, (4) component architecture revolution, (5) interaction paradigm innovation.
entries.rationale: mathcrew has a solid ontology-first foundation (BackendOntology + FrontendOntology, 5 islands, 7 interaction primitives, LEARN infrastructure). But the frontend 3D rendering is classroom-basic (shadows off, no post-processing), interaction is keyboard-only (E/Q), content delivery is chalkboard-centric. The gap between ontology sophistication and frontend execution quality is the primary concern.

ontology.strength: Well-structured BackendOntology (12 DATA entities, 17 link types, 6 derived properties, 5 decision rules) + FrontendOntology (4 views, 2 agent surfaces, 7 interaction primitives, 5 object types)
ontology.gap: FrontendOntology declarations are rich but runtime rendering does not reflect the declared sophistication. Ontology declares 'teaching surface' but runtime delivers basic geometry.

rendering.current: R3F 9.5 + Three.js 0.183 + WebGPU/WebGL fallback. ProceduralSky (basic gradient + points), TSL fog, PostProcessing (MRT bloom/GTAO/vignette/FXAA — mostly disabled). Quality presets: classroom (everything off) vs immersive (vignette+FXAA only).
rendering.gap: No stylized shading (toon/cel), no water/ocean, no weather, no dynamic lighting for learning phases, no particle effects for math visualization. Islands are flat cylinders with solid colors.

interaction.current: Keyboard-only: WASD movement, E pickup/interact, Q drop, R reset, Enter advance. 7 interaction primitives registered but 3 still 'designed' or 'undesigned'.
interaction.gap: No touch support, no gamepad, no gesture recognition, no accessibility (screen reader + 3D), no multi-modal feedback (audio/haptic). Mobile/tablet unusable.

contentDelivery.current: 5-phase Kolb cycle per scene (motivate→explain→demonstrate→practice→assess). NPC narration via Board3D chalkboard. Step-by-step linear progression.
contentDelivery.gap: No adaptive branching within scenes, no multi-representation (CRA: concrete→representational→abstract), no animated math explanations (3Blue1Brown-style), no audio narration, no hint escalation system.

components.current: MathWorld.tsx (770 lines) orchestrates everything. Per-scene components (PetShareScene, DualPetShareScene, etc). State managed via useState chains.
components.gap: No state machine for scene flow, no ECS, no lazy scene loading, no micro-scene architecture. MathWorld.tsx is becoming a God component.

stack.current: NestJS + MongoDB + React + R3F + Rapier. No xAPI, no LRS, no adaptive engine, no spaced repetition, no IRT calibration.
stack.gap: Backend stores attempts but doesn't do adaptive computation beyond simple mastery%. No learning analytics beyond basic counts.

decisions.RQ1: external+context7 — 3D rendering: TSL toon shading, procedural terrain/water, WebGPU compute for math viz, R3F v9 patterns
decisions.RQ2: external — Interaction: embodied cognition, touch/gesture, accessibility-first 3D, multi-modal feedback
decisions.RQ3: external — Pedagogy: CRA digital implementation, scaffolding patterns, game-based learning evidence, Korean math curriculum
decisions.RQ4: external — Edu-tech: xAPI/LRS, BKT/IRT/FSRS adaptive engines, AI tutoring patterns, learning analytics
decisions.RQ5: external+context7 — Architecture: ECS for R3F, XState scene flow, micro-scenes, diegetic UI, R3F v9 advanced
decisions.internal-01: §DC5-05 design principles (DDD/DRY/OCP/PECS) — applied to component architecture revolution
decisions.internal-02: §DC5-06 ontology primitives — applied to interaction primitive plugin system
decisions.internal-03: §DC5-04 3-phase journey — applied to implementation roadmap (Golden Tables→Kinetics→AI-First)
decisions.internal-04: §DC-SIG-03 builder scope — frontend ontology must include application+agent+scenario surfaces
decisions.internal-05: §FDE-09 expanded builder surfaces — agent-driven content creation and evaluation

requirements.description: 3D rendering quality leap: stylized shading, procedural environments, math-specific visualization
Multi-device interaction: touch + keyboard + gamepad + accessibility
Evidence-based content delivery: CRA progression, scaffolding, multi-modal
Adaptive learning engine: BKT/IRT/FSRS beyond simple mastery%
Component architecture overhaul: decompose MathWorld.tsx God component
Learning analytics: xAPI traces, teacher dashboard with actionable insights
Performance: 30fps on Chromebooks with new rendering features
Preserve ontology-first authority chain through all changes

questions.text: What TSL/WebGPU techniques achieve Pixar-quality stylized rendering while maintaining 30fps on Chromebooks?
What interaction paradigms beyond keyboard E/Q enable embodied math learning in 3D?
How to implement CRA (Concrete-Representational-Abstract) progression in a digital 3D math environment?
What education-specialized tech components (xAPI, BKT, IRT, FSRS) integrate with TypeScript/Bun stack?
What component architecture patterns (ECS, state machines, micro-scenes) best decompose a 3D educational world?
